canonical_definition: Based on the provided data, PDDL is a benchmark designed to evaluate LLM agents. Its stated purpose is to assess agent performance on planning tasks within PDDL-style environments. One paper specifies that the benchmark covers "diverse use cases in web, embodied, tool use and game scenarios" (AgentSquare: Automatic LLM Agent Search in Modular Design Space). In addition to its focus on planning, PDDL is also used as a measurement instrument for the construct of Decision-making.
papers: AgentRefine: Enhancing Agent Generalization through Refinement Tuning
AgentSquare: Automatic LLM Agent Search in Modular Design Space
Non-myopic Generation of Language Models for Reasoning and Planning

definitions.text: A benchmark for evaluating LLM agents on planning tasks expressed in PDDL-style environments.
definitions.paper: AgentSquare: Automatic LLM Agent Search in Modular Design Space

relationships.paper: AgentRefine: Enhancing Agent Generalization through Refinement Tuning
